page: Microsoft Azure D8lds_v6 is a compute-oriented server belonging to the Dldsv6 family, running on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor. It features an x86_64 architecture powered by an Intel Xeon 8573C processor with 8 vCPUs, 4 physical cores, and 2 threads per core. The dedicated CPU operates at a clock speed of 4.2 GHz and includes a 260 MB L3 cache. It is equipped with 16.0 GB of system memory, yielding a low memory-to-core ratio of 2.0 GB per vCPU. Local storage is bundled with a 472 GB disk, and the instance supports Premium Storage. No GPUs or complimentary public IPv4 addresses are included.

Performance benchmarks indicate strong single-threaded capabilities. Single-threaded gzip compression reached 31.52 GB/sec, and Geekbench single-core testing scored 2166.0, both placing in the top 25% tier. Multi-core performance is moderate, with a Geekbench multi-core score of 8360.0 and a stress-ng multi-core score of 7990.55. Memory bandwidth is measured at 621.35 GB/sec for cached small blocks, dropping to 67.27 GB/sec for uncached large blocks. For artificial intelligence workloads, the server demonstrates strong text generation performance on small 135M LLM models at 313.4 tokens/sec, but exhibits weak performance on medium 7B models, dropping to 7.97 tokens/sec.

The primary tradeoff of the D8lds_v6 is its low memory density, which limits its applicability for memory-intensive databases or large-scale data processing. However, this resource allocation makes the server highly cost-efficient for compute-bound workloads that do not require substantial RAM. It is well-positioned for tasks such as software compilation, text processing, web serving, and lightweight machine learning inference.
description: Microsoft Azure D8lds_v6 is an x86_64 compute-oriented server featuring 8 vCPUs and 16.0 GB of RAM, running on an Intel Xeon 8573C processor at 4.2 GHz. Built on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor, this instance provides a dedicated CPU allocation with 4 physical cores and a 260 MB L3 cache. It includes 472 GB of local storage and supports Premium Storage, though it lacks GPU accelerators. Benchmarks show strong single-core performance, with Geekbench single-core scoring 2166.0 and single-threaded gzip compression reaching 31.52 GB/sec. However, its low memory-to-core ratio of 2.0 GB per vCPU represents a tradeoff for memory-heavy applications. This server is cost-efficient for compute-focused workloads that require minimal memory, making it suitable for software compilation, text processing, static web serving, and small-model LLM inference.
og_description: Microsoft Azure D8lds_v6 is an x86_64 server with 8 vCPUs, 16 GB RAM, and a 4.2 GHz Intel Xeon processor. It features 472 GB of local storage and delivers strong single-core performance.
meta_description: Microsoft Azure D8lds_v6 is an x86_64 server featuring 8 vCPUs, 16 GB RAM, 472 GB local storage, and a 4.2 GHz Intel Xeon processor.
tagline: An x86_64 compute-focused server featuring high clock-speed Intel processors, local storage, and a low-memory footprint for cost-efficient processing.

16.0 GB of system memory and 472 GB of local storage
Strong single-core performance in gzip compression and Geekbench
Low memory-to-core ratio of 2.0 GB per vCPU
Suitable for software compilation, text processing, and web serving
